policy_statement: Coastal adaptation strategy: develop and implement coastal zone policy/design/management plan; develop an integrated coastal management plan and early warning system with training; promote disaster risk management and preparedness; rehabilitate and protect wetlands and mangroves; and climate-proof new coastal infrastructure investments.
mechanism_description: Develops and implements coastal management planning, early warning and preparedness activities, rehabilitates and protects wetlands and mangroves, and climate-proofs new coastal infrastructure to reduce coastal climate hazard risks and impacts.
primary_causal_pathway: Climate hazard → ↓ exposure/sensitivity (via coastal planning + early warning + protective buffers + resilient infrastructure) → ↓ climate risk/impacts
causal_mechanism_detail: (1) Changes coastal governance and land/infrastructure decisions by implementing integrated coastal zone management, preparedness and early warning, restoring/protecting mangroves and wetlands, and climate-proofing new coastal infrastructure. (2) These measures mediate impacts by improving hazard monitoring/response, steering development away from high-risk zones, dissipating wave energy and attenuating storm surge via coastal ecosystems, stabilizing shorelines, and increasing built asset robustness. (3) The climate-relevant outcome is reduced mortality, economic loss, service disruption, and ecosystem damage from sea-level rise and extreme coastal events.
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is reduced exposure and damages to people, assets, and ecosystems from coastal hazards (e.g., storm surge, flooding, erosion) through improved preparedness, protective nature-based buffers, and climate-resilient coastal infrastructure.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: interventions target coastal hazard management (planning, warnings, protective ecosystems, resilient infrastructure) leading to reduced exposure/sensitivity and thus reduced climate impacts.  
2) Why Adaptation over alternatives: while mangroves/wetlands can sequester carbon (Nature-Based Solutions) and planning can improve efficiency, the central objective and largest effect across policies using this mechanism is hazard risk reduction, which is Adaptation.  
3) Edge cases applied: nature-based components may yield mitigation co-benefits (carbon storage) and resource co-benefits (fisheries, water quality), but these are typically secondary to coastal protection and preparedness in this mechanism definition.  
4) Applies to ALL instances: any policy matching “coastal adaptation planning + preparedness/early warning + nature-based coastal protection + climate-proofing coastal infrastructure” is inherently oriented to reducing climate hazard impacts, so Adaptation remains the primary category.  
5) Secondary subtype assignment (e.g., ecosystem-based adaptation vs engineered protection vs early warning) is deferred to policy-level evidence and does not change the primary category.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: If a policy instance is dominated by mangrove/wetland restoration explicitly for carbon sequestration (with coastal protection only incidental), it could shift toward Nature-Based Solutions; however, under this canonical mechanism the stated dominant intent/effect is coastal hazard risk reduction, so Adaptation is primary.
key_indicators: “develop and implement coastal zone policy, design, and management plan”; “integrated management plan for coastal zone management”; “early warning system”; “training and capacity development”; “Promote and implement disaster risk management… (especially disaster preparedness)”; “rehabilitation and protection of wetlands and mangroves”; “program for climate-proofing new investments in infrastructure”
co_benefits: Biodiversity conservation and fisheries support from wetland/mangrove protection; improved water quality and sanitation reliability via resilient sewers/water supplies; reduced non-climate disaster losses through broader disaster risk management capacity.
instance_edge_case_notes: This package includes elements that could also be coded as infrastructure-investment (climate-proofing infrastructure) and information/disaster management systems (early warning, training), but the dominant framing is an integrated coastal zone management plan/strategy encompassing multiple instruments.
